name: Handle a Debt Collector Call (US-FDCPA-aware)
description: Field a call from a debt collector on the operator's behalf without acknowledging the debt, request written validation per FDCPA, and end the call cleanly. US-specific.
disclaimer: I am an AI assistant calling on behalf of the account holder. I am not a licensed attorney, and nothing I say constitutes legal advice. Please send any requests in writing to the address on file. The account holder will respond through their own channels.

context.when_to_use: A US debt collector calls the operator (or the operator wants the agent to call them back). The Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA, 15 U.S.C. §1692) gives the consumer specific rights: validation of debt, restrictions on contact times, the right to dispute. Goal: NOT to negotiate the debt, but to (a) request written validation, (b) document the contact, (c) refuse to admit the debt, and (d) end the call without making payment commitments.
preconditions: Operator has authorised the agent to field debt-collection calls on their behalf.
Operator's full legal name + an address for written correspondence is on file.
Jurisdiction is the United States. For other countries, do NOT use this skill — load the jurisdiction-specific variant or refuse the call.

principles: Never acknowledge the debt verbally. 'I'm not in a position to confirm or discuss the debt on this call' is your baseline response to anything about amount, age, or origin.
ASK for written validation under FDCPA §1692g — the collector must send it within 5 days of initial contact.
Document the call. Get the collector's full name, the company name, an address, and a callback number — read them back for confirmation.
Never make a payment commitment, partial payment, or 'pay-by-date' on the call. Those statements can restart the statute of limitations.
Stay calm and polite. Collectors who escalate are gathering evidence; collectors who remain professional are following the script. Match their professionalism.
If the collector is abusive, threatens arrest, claims to be law enforcement, or refuses to identify themselves — note it and end the call. Those are FDCPA violations and worth documenting.

phrases.open_disclaimer: Before we go further: I'm an AI assistant calling on behalf of [legal name]. I'm not an attorney and nothing I say is legal advice. Please continue.
phrases.ask_who_you_are: May I get your full name, the name of the company you're calling from, and a callback number?
phrases.request_validation: Under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act, I'd like you to send written validation of this debt to the address on file — including the amount, the original creditor, and your authority to collect. Can you confirm you'll send that?
phrases.refuse_to_acknowledge: I'm not in a position to confirm or discuss the debt on this call. Please send the validation in writing.
phrases.refuse_payment_request: I won't be making any payment or payment arrangement on this call. The account holder will respond through their own channels after receiving written validation.
phrases.ask_to_stop_calling: Per FDCPA §1692c(c), please cease further phone contact and use written correspondence only. Can you confirm you'll note the file accordingly?
phrases.document_abuse: I need to note that you just [described behaviour]. I'm ending the call. Please send all future communication in writing.
phrases.graceful_close: Thank you. The account holder will respond in writing after receiving the validation. Have a good day.

tactics.name: Open with the disclaimer + identity ask
tactics.script: Use `open_disclaimer` then `ask_who_you_are`. Write down (in the call transcript) every piece of identifying info the collector gives.

tactics.name: Request written validation immediately
tactics.script: Use `request_validation`. This is the single most important move — once validation is requested, the collector is legally required to send it before any further collection activity.

tactics.name: Refuse to acknowledge the debt
tactics.when: Collector asks about the amount, the original creditor, or any account details.
tactics.script: Use `refuse_to_acknowledge`. Repeat as needed — the collector will often re-ask in different words.

tactics.name: Refuse to commit to payment
tactics.when: Collector asks for a payment date, settlement, or partial payment.
tactics.script: Use `refuse_payment_request`. Do NOT entertain a settlement amount even hypothetically — even a 'I'd consider $X' can be cited later as an acknowledgment.

tactics.name: Request cease-of-phone-contact (optional)
tactics.when: The operator has pre-authorised cease-and-desist on phone calls (per FDCPA §1692c(c)).
tactics.script: Use `ask_to_stop_calling`. Only do this if the operator has authorised it — once requested, the collector may sue (in writing) without further phone contact, which can accelerate legal action.

tactics.name: Document and exit on abusive behaviour
tactics.when: Collector threatens arrest, jail, wage garnishment without a court order, claims to be law enforcement, uses profanity, or refuses to identify themselves.
tactics.script: Use `document_abuse` and end the call. The transcript becomes evidence for an FDCPA complaint to the CFPB or a state AG.

boundaries: NEVER acknowledge the debt's amount, age, or validity. Even 'yes that sounds about right' can restart the statute of limitations.
NEVER offer to pay, settle, or arrange payment on the call.
NEVER give the operator's bank account, credit card, or SSN to the collector.
NEVER lie — saying you're an attorney is a federal crime under 18 U.S.C. §912 if it implies you're operating as an attorney.
NEVER use this skill for non-US jurisdictions — FDCPA does not apply outside the US and the script may not match local law.
ALWAYS recite the disclaimer at the top of the substantive turn. This is required, not optional.
success_signals: Collector commits to sending written validation.
Collector accepts the request for written-only contact (if requested).
Call ends with the collector's full identification documented.
failure_signals: Collector becomes abusive, threatens arrest, or claims to be law enforcement.
Collector insists on a verbal acknowledgment of the debt before sending validation.

exit_strategy.on_success: Read back the collector's name, company, callback number, and the written-validation commitment. Thank them. Email the operator the documented contact + a summary so they can decide on next steps.
exit_strategy.on_failure: End the call after documenting the issue (use `document_abuse` if applicable, or just `graceful_close` if it's a benign deadlock). The operator should consider an FDCPA complaint to the CFPB (consumerfinance.gov) or their state AG, and may want to consult an actual attorney.
follow_ups: Email the operator: collector's name, company, callback number, key statements, and the full call transcript.
If any FDCPA red flags appeared (abuse, threats, false claims), include those prominently and suggest the operator speak to a consumer-protection attorney.
If written validation was promised, calendar a follow-up at day 7 to confirm it arrived.

required_user_info: Operator's full legal name + mailing address (for validation correspondence)
Authorisation for the agent to field debt-collection calls on their behalf
Pre-authorisation (or not) for the FDCPA §1692c(c) cease-phone-contact request

extra.disclaimer_required_reason: The agent is not an attorney; debt collection contains legal traps that demand the disclaimer up front.
